steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The passage moves from describing what people initially assume — a real hole with sculptures — to what is actually true: an illusion of depth. The transition must signal this shift from false impression to reality.

steps.title: Select the transition word that marks opposition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contradiction: the second sentence directly reverses the assumption stated in the first. The blank needs a transition word signaling that the truth is the opposite of what was just described.
  • Choice A — ‘Additionally,’: signals that more information of the same kind follows, so it fails because the second sentence contradicts, not extends, the first. ✘
  • Choice B — ‘Next,’: signals a sequence of steps or events, so it fails because no chronological process is being narrated. ✘
  • Choice C — ‘As a result,’: signals a cause-and-effect consequence, so it fails because the illusion is not caused by the assumption — it is what corrects it. ✘
  • Choice D — ‘On the contrary,’: signals that the following statement is the direct opposite of what was just said, so it fits — Tanner created only an illusion, not a real hole. ✓
This confirms D is correct.
